qa_pairs.question: How do I manage access permissions for a shared Globus collection, including adding users or groups and setting write permissions?
qa_pairs.answer: To manage access for a shared Globus collection, first, you'll see the current access list after the collection is created. Click the "Add Permissions — Share With" button to add people or groups. You can specify a path within the share (e.g., "/Subdir-01/") or use "/" for the entire collection. Then, select to share with a "User" (by email, name, or Globus username) or a "Group" (by group name or UUID). To enable write permissions, click the 'write' checkbox in the form. You can also add or remove write permissions from the access list by clicking the checkbox next to the name under the 'WRITE' column. To remove users or groups, click the 'x' at the end of their information in the access list.

qa_pairs.question: What are some important security considerations to keep in mind when sharing files with Globus?
qa_pairs.answer: When sharing files with Globus, it's important to ensure you have permission to share the data if you are not the owner. Verify that you are sharing with only the intended individuals; using their email address is the recommended method. If sharing with a group you do not control, ensure you trust the owner, as they might add unauthorized people. If granting write access, make sure to have backups of important files not on the shared endpoint, as users may delete or overwrite files. Lastly, it is highly recommended to restrict sharing to a subdirectory rather than your top-level home directory.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of Globus groups, and how can a user create a new group?
qa_pairs.answer: Globus groups provide an easy way to manage permissions for sharing with multiple users simultaneously. When a group is created, it can be used from the sharing interface to control access for numerous users. To create a new group, click the 'Groups' button on the left sidebar of the Alliance Globus portal. Then, click the 'Create New Group' button on the top right. In the 'Create New Group' window, enter the group's name and description, and select whether it is visible only to group members (private) or all Globus users, before clicking 'Create Group'.
